name: Mom's Divinity
recipeInstructions:
Cook only on a dry day!
In a heavy-bottom pan over medium heat, boil the syrup of sugar, water, and Karo until it reaches nearly done (about 10 minutes); it will spin a thread to the pot. Remove from heat.
Beat egg white with a pinch of salt until stiff peaks form. In a large bowl, add the hot syrup to the beaten egg white while mixing at high speed. If the syrup starts to harden, pour in a little more. When the mixture becomes stiffer and warmer, add it in larger streams.
Add vanilla extract to the candy. Stir by hand until the texture dulls (this may take a long time). Allow to cool before adding nuts (if added too soon, the heat can cause the oil to separate from the nuts, preventing the candy from hardening). Drop by tablespoons onto waxed paper.
To expand the recipe, use at least 2 cups of sugar; if you quadruple the recipe, add an extra egg white, especially if using small eggs. You may also try stirring in 3 oz. of broken white chocolate before dropping the candy (it will not melt).
